zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett是Robert Gobbett的女兒，於1519年出生在英國諾福爾可迪可萊包。她於1544年10月26日與Nicholas Fylby在英國英格蘭諾福爾可迪可萊包結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1586年11月20日在她的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett是Robert Gobbett的女儿，于1519年出生在英国諾福爾可迪可萊包。她于1544年10月26日与Nicholas Fylby在英国英格兰諾福爾可迪可萊包结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1586年11月20日在她的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gobbett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname, perhaps from Middle English (Old French) gobet ‘lump, morsel’ or from Middle English go bet ‘go better’; compare Walter Gobiforn (‘go before’), 1212 in Pleas (Yorks); John Gobisid’ (‘go beside’), 1379 in Yorks Poll Tax , and Golightly .

variants.name: Yorks Poll Tax
meanings: 1  English: locative name from D'Ovesdale Manor in Litlington (Cambs), or occasionally from Dovedale (Derbys), as in the case of Agnes Doghdale, 1379 in   Yorks Poll Tax  .
2 Irish: in Ireland, sometimes adopted for Ó Dubhdáleithe, see also Dudley .

variants.name: Godbold
meanings: English (Suffolk): from the Middle English and Old French personal name Godebald, Godbaud, a medieval French form of the ancient Germanic name Godebald, composed of the elements gōd ‘good’ or god ‘god’ + bald ‘bold, brave’.

variants.name: Golightly
meanings: English (Durham): nickname, perhaps for a messenger, from Middle English gō(n) ‘to go’ (Old English gān) + lihtly ‘lightly, swiftly’ (Old English lēoht(līc)).
Scottish and English: altered form of Scottish Galletly, a variant of Irish Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett was born in 1519, in Dickleburgh, Norfolk, England as the daughter of Robert Gobbett. She married Nicholas Fylby on 26 October 1544, in Dickleburgh, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 20 November 1586, in her hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett est née en 1519, à Dickleburgh, Norfolk, Angleterre, fille de Robert Gobbett. Elle a épousé Nicholas Fylby le 26 octobre 1544, à Dickleburgh, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 20 novembre 1586, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett wurde 1519 in Dickleburgh, Norfolk, England, als Tochter von Robert Gobbett geboren. Sie hat am 26. Oktober 1544 in Dickleburgh, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Nicholas Fylby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 20. November 1586 im Alter von 67 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett è nata il nel 1519 nel Dickleburgh, Norfolk, England come figlia di Robert Gobbett. Lei sposato Nicholas Fylby il 26 ottobre 1544, nel Dickleburgh, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 20 novembre 1586, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbettは1519年にイギリスノーフォルクディックレブルグでRobert Gobbettの娘として生まれた。1544年10月26日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクディックレブルグでNicholas Fylbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1586年11月20日に故郷で67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett(은)는 1519년 영국 노르폴크 딕레버그h에서 Robert Gobbett의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1544년 10월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 딕레버그h에서 Nicholas Fylby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1586년 11월 20일 그녀의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gobbett родилась в 1519 г. в местности дикклебург, норфолк, Англия. Её отца звали Robert Gobbett. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas Fylby 26 октября 1544 г. в местности дикклебург, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 20 ноября 1586 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.
